compiled_state.author: RebarBotNava
compiled_state.description:
## Reproduction Steps
1. Run `rebar review-plan <id>` (attested, signable) while ANY other rebar ticket-write is in flight (e.g. several concurrent background reviews, a waiter loop, another claim/edit) — enough contention to leave a stale/contended `$(git rev-parse --git-common-dir)/worktrees/-tickets-tracker/index.lock`.
2. The review runs its full multi-pass LLM pipeline (~8-12 min) and computes verdict=PASS, source=attested, signable=True.
3. The attestation SIGN step tries to commit the SIGNATURE event to the tickets branch and fails on the lock.

## Observed
The returned verdict dict is verdict=PASS with:
```
signature: {'signed': False, 'error': "Error: git commit failed while holding lock: fatal: Unable to create '.../.git/worktrees/-tickets-tracker/index.lock': File exists. Another git process seems to be running in this repository, or the lock file may be stale"}
```
The CLI exits 0 (PASS). But NO attestation is persisted on the ticket, so a subsequent `rebar claim <id>` FAILS the plan-review claim gate ('run the plan review to earn an attestation'). The only recovery is to re-run the ENTIRE ~10-min billable review. Observed live on ticket gnomish-nosophobic-arawana during epic jira-reb-687.

## Why this is major impact
- **Expensive, billable work is silently thrown away.** A full multi-pass LLM plan-review (minutes + real API cost) succeeds, but its sole durable product — the signed attestation that unlocks `claim` — is lost to a transient, recoverable git-lock condition.
- **Exit 0 + PASS masks the failure.** The signature failure is reported only inside `result.signature.error`; the process exits 0 and prints PASS, so an automated caller (or a human reading the verdict) reasonably believes the ticket is now claimable when it is not. The next `claim` fails confusingly ('no attestation') with no obvious link back to the swallowed sign error.
- **No cheap recovery path.** There is no way to re-persist the already-computed PASS verdict; you must re-run the whole review, doubling cost/latency and risking a DIFFERENT verdict on re-run (non-determinism).

## Root cause (direction)
The sign step commits the SIGNATURE event under the tickets-branch worktree index lock; a contended/stale `index.lock` makes `git commit` fail, and the failure is caught and recorded into `signature.error` but NOT (a) retried, (b) recovered by clearing a provably-stale lock, or (c) surfaced as a non-zero exit / loud CLI error. The attestation write is not resilient to the ordinary concurrency the rest of rebar's store already tolerates (writes auto-commit+auto-push under this lock constantly).

## Expected / graceful handling (design first)
- **Retry the signature commit** with bounded backoff on a lock/commit failure (the store's other writers already contend on this lock; the sign write should too).
- **Detect and clear a provably-stale `index.lock`** (no live git process / lock older than a threshold) before failing — the same recovery a human does by hand.
- **Fail LOUD, not silent**: if the verdict is PASS+signable but signing fails, the CLI should exit non-zero (or a distinct code) and print the sign error prominently — never exit 0/PASS while the attestation was NOT written.
- **Offer a cheap re-sign** of an already-computed, still-valid PASS verdict (persist the computed manifest so a follow-up `sign`/`review-plan` can re-attest without re-running all LLM passes) — so a transient lock costs a retry, not a full re-review.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] A signature-commit failure on a contended/stale lock is retried (bounded) and/or recovers by clearing a provably-stale lock; a transient lock no longer loses the attestation.
- [ ] When a PASS+signable review fails to persist its signature, the CLI exits NON-ZERO and prints the sign error prominently (no silent exit-0/PASS with an unwritten attestation).
- [ ] There is a cheap path to (re)persist the signature for an already-computed, still-valid PASS verdict without re-running the LLM passes.
- [ ] A regression test simulates a signature-commit failure and asserts (a) retry/recovery persists the attestation, or (b) a loud non-zero failure — never silent loss.
- [ ] make lint && make typecheck && make test green.
compiled_state.title: plan-review computes PASS but silently loses the attestation when the signature commit fails (transient git index.lock) — expensive review wasted, claim still blocked
